title: dubautia laxa subsp. hirsuta
intro: Dubautia laxa subsp. hirsuta, also known as Hairy Dubautia, is a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family that is native to Hawaii. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. It is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, treating wounds, and preventing infections, making it a valuable resource for herbal remedies. The herbal preparations of Dubautia laxa subsp. hirsuta include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
